title: Weekly Market Report | Jun 15-19, 2026
report:
# Weekly Market Report | June 15-19, 2026

---

## Japan Equity Markets — A Historic Week

The Nikkei 225 smashed through 70,000 for the first time in its 77-year history, propelled by the landmark US-Iran peace accord and a historic semiconductor rally.

**Nikkei 225 Daily Closes:**
- Mon 6/16: ~68,800 (gap up on weekend Iran peace news)
- Tue 6/17: 69,404 (briefly touched 70,000 intraday — first ever)
- Wed 6/18: 68,362 (-1,042 pts, FOMC result + AI rally pause)
- Thu 6/19: 71,053 (+2,691 / +3.9%). US-Iran peace signed at Versailles. US lifts Iran naval blockade. Oil drops below $80. Massive risk-on.
- Fri 6/20: 70,704 (-349 / -0.5%). Mild profit-taking, held above 70K.

**Weekly: 66,329 to 70,704 — +4,375 pts (+6.6%)**

TOPIX: +4.0% weekly. TSE Prime trading value hit record levels of Y18-25T daily.

---

## Headline Events

**US-Iran Peace Accord (Jun 18, Versailles)**

Presidents Trump and Pezeshkian signed a permanent peace framework at the Palace of Versailles. The US immediately lifted its naval blockade on Iran. WTI crude plunged below $80/bbl, triggering a global risk-on wave. The Nikkei surged +2,691 points on this single day — one of the largest one-day gains in history.

**FOMC (Jun 16-17) — Chair Warsh's Debut**

Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75%. The dot plot was hawkish (9 members favor further hikes in 2026), but new Chair Warsh signaled in his press conference that 'the next move is more likely a cut.' 2-year Treasury yield dropped to 4.01%. December hike probability plunged from 90% to 60%.

**Nikkei 225 Crosses 70,000 — Then 71,000**

First-ever intraday break above 70,000 on June 17. Closed above 71,000 for the first time on June 19 at 71,053. A 77-year milestone.

**Kioxia Holdings Hits Y100,000**

NAND flash memory giant Kioxia reached 100,000 yen per share for the first time. Market cap crossed Y50T. Strong FY2026 Q1 guidance drove a 7-day winning streak.

---

## Theme Performance Rankings

### Rising Top 5

**#1: AI Semiconductor +18.5%**
Post-FOMC relief + Iran peace risk-on explosion. Advantest, Tokyo Electron, Lasertec surged daily. SK Hynix HBM4E sample shipment news boosted memory sentiment. Murata Manufacturing +12.38% on Thursday alone.

**#2: MLCC / Electronic Components +14.2%**
Continued from last week's momentum. AI chip component demand + SpaceX Starlink satellite production demand.

**#3: Semiconductor Equipment +12.8%**
Tokyo Electron, DISCO, SCREEN HD led. Kioxia's 100K breakthrough lifted entire sector sentiment.

**#4: Defense & Aerospace +9.3%**
Brief selloff on Iran peace quickly reversed. Japan's defense budget expansion plans continue regardless. Mitsubishi Heavy, IHI strong.

**#5: Next-Gen Semiconductor +8.7%**
Sub-2nm process technology and advanced packaging. Rapidus Hokkaido fab progress reports provided additional catalyst.

### Falling Bottom 5

**#1: Rare Earth / Resources -3.2%**
Oil and commodity price declines on Iran peace. Sumitomo Metal Mining -7.04%. Geopolitical premium evaporated.

**#2: Crypto / Web3 -2.8%**
Bitcoin at ~$66,100. Spot BTC ETFs recorded 5 consecutive weeks of net outflows.

**#3: Quantum Computing -1.9%**
Continued profit-taking. No near-term commercialization catalysts.

**#4: Biotech / Drug Discovery -1.5%**
Daiichi Sankyo -5.95%. Thin news flow on drug approvals this week.

**#5: Regional Banks -0.8%**
Rate hike expectations moderated after Warsh's 'next move is a cut' comment.

---

## US Markets

**June 18 close:**
- Dow: 51,564.70 (+0.14%)
- S&P 500: 7,500.58 (+1.08%)
- Nasdaq: 26,517.93 (+1.91%)

Iran peace drove oil lower, easing inflation fears and funneling capital into tech. Micron (MU) price targets raised to $1,500 by Deutsche Bank and TD Cowen.

---

## Key Economic Data

- Japan CPI (May, released 6/19): Core 1.4% (steady). Contained inflation reduces BoJ rate hike urgency
- US CPI (May): Headline 4.2%, Core 2.9%. Energy factor transitory. Iran peace oil decline supports future CPI drops
- FOMC: 3.50-3.75% hold. Dot plot hawkish but Warsh hints at cuts

---

## Watch List for Next Week (Jun 22-26)

- SpaceX (SPCX) second week of trading — stabilization or volatility
- BoJ May meeting minutes — detailed rate path discussion
- US-China semiconductor export controls — potential new restrictions
- Rapidus Hokkaido fab update
- Quarter-end fund rebalancing — selling pressure risk

---

## StockWaveJP Editorial View

Nikkei 70,000 is historic but this week's surge was heavily driven by the Iran peace external catalyst. Quarter-end rebalancing pressure could create headwinds next week.

However, the AI rally structure is fundamentally sound. Kioxia at 100K, Murata +12.38% — real demand across the semiconductor value chain. Any correction likely finds strong support at 69,000-70,000.
